We are given a parametric surface defined by the equations:

$$
x(u, v) = a \sin(u) \cos(v)
$$
$$
y(u, v) = a \sin(u) \sin(v)
$$
$$
z(u, v) = b \cos(u)
$$

where $ 0 \leq u \leq \pi $ and $ 0 \leq v < 2\pi $.

---

### **Part 1: Surface Area Calculation**

The surface area $ A $ of a parametric surface defined by $ \vec{r}(u, v) = (x(u, v), y(u, v), z(u, v)) $ is given by:

$$
A = \iint_{D} \left\| \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} \right\| \, du \, dv
$$

We compute the partial derivatives:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} = \left( a \cos(u) \cos(v), a \cos(u) \sin(v), -b \sin(u) \right)
$$
$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} = \left( -a \sin(u) \sin(v), a \sin(u) \cos(v), 0 \right)
$$

Now compute the cross product:

$$
\frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial u} \times \frac{\partial \vec{r}}{\partial v} =
\begin{vmatrix}
\mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\
a \cos(u) \cos(v) & a \cos(u) \sin(v) & -b \sin(u) \\
-a \sin(u) \sin(v) & a \sin(u) \cos(v) & 0
\end{vmatrix}
$$

Expanding the determinant:
